In `mailbox.json` (external services), exactly 1 email in draft to no one ("to_addr": empty or NULL)

In `mailbox.json` (external services), the draft email has "body_text":"Dear [Payer Billing/Appeals Contact Name],

I am writing on behalf of Pathfinder Billing and Coding to formally dispute the payment amounts received for CPT 11241 under Payer ID A4789P. The following claims were paid below the contracted rate with no adjustment reason code provided:

HIC-19887899 | 2025-12-01 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-19887900 | 2025-12-01 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-19887901 | 2025-12-03 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-25124778 | 2025-12-10 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-25124779 | 2025-12-12 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-25124780 | 2025-12-15 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-26877757 | 2025-12-29 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10
HIC-26877758 | 2026-01-02 | Contracted: $75 | Paid: $65 | Variance: $10
HIC-26877795 | 2026-01-05 | Contracted: $75 | Paid: $65 | Variance: $10

Total outstanding variance: $90

We request review and remittance of the outstanding balance within [X] Business Days. Please confirm receipt of this dispute and provide a reference number for tracking.

Joe Malfroy | Pathfinder Billing and Coding | rcm@pathfinder.com"
verifier_code:
from pathlib import Path
import json
import re

def verify(workspace_path, external_services_path=None):
    # The rubric specifies a very specific body_text in a draft email in mailbox.json (external services).
    # We need to find a draft email whose body_text matches the expected content.
    
    # Expected body text from the rubric
    expected_body = (
        "Dear [Payer Billing/Appeals Contact Name],\n\n"
        "I am writing on behalf of Pathfinder Billing and Coding to formally dispute the payment amounts received for CPT 11241 under Payer ID A4789P. "
        "The following claims were paid below the contracted rate with no adjustment reason code provided:\n\n"
        "HIC-19887899 | 2025-12-01 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-19887900 | 2025-12-01 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-19887901 | 2025-12-03 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-25124778 | 2025-12-10 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-25124779 | 2025-12-12 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-25124780 | 2025-12-15 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-26877757 | 2025-12-29 | Contracted: $80 | Paid: $70 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-26877758 | 2026-01-02 | Contracted: $75 | Paid: $65 | Variance: $10\n"
        "HIC-26877795 | 2026-01-05 | Contracted: $75 | Paid: $65 | Variance: $10\n\n"
        "Total outstanding variance: $90\n\n"
        "We request review and remittance of the outstanding balance within [X] Business Days. "
        "Please confirm receipt of this dispute and provide a reference number for tracking.\n\n"
        "Joe Malfroy | Pathfinder Billing and Coding | rcm@pathfinder.com"
    )

    # Key fragments to check for partial credit
    key_fragments = [
        ("Payer Billing/Appeals Contact Name", "Salutation placeholder"),
        ("Pathfinder Billing and Coding", "Company name"),
        ("CPT 11241", "CPT code"),
        ("Payer ID A4789P", "Payer ID"),
        ("HIC-19887899", "Claim HIC-19887899"),
        ("HIC-19887900", "Claim HIC-19887900"),
        ("HIC-19887901", "Claim HIC-19887901"),
        ("HIC-25124778", "Claim HIC-25124778"),
        ("HIC-25124779", "Claim HIC-25124779"),
        ("HIC-25124780", "Claim HIC-25124780"),
        ("HIC-26877757", "Claim HIC-26877757"),
        ("HIC-26877758", "Claim HIC-26877758"),
        ("HIC-26877795", "Claim HIC-26877795"),
        ("2025-12-01", "DOS 2025-12-01"),
        ("2025-12-03", "DOS 2025-12-03"),
        ("2025-12-10", "DOS 2025-12-10"),
        ("2025-12-12", "DOS 2025-12-12"),
        ("2025-12-15", "DOS 2025-12-15"),
        ("2025-12-29", "DOS 2025-12-29"),
        ("2026-01-02", "DOS 2026-01-02"),
        ("2026-01-05", "DOS 2026-01-05"),
        ("Total outstanding variance: $90", "Total variance $90"),
        ("Joe Malfroy", "Joe Malfroy signature"),
        ("rcm@pathfinder.com", "Email address"),
    ]

    # Try to find mailbox.json
    paths_to_check = []
    if external_services_path:
        paths_to_check.append(Path(external_services_path) / 'mailbox.json')
    paths_to_check.append(Path(workspace_path) / 'mailbox.json')

    mailbox_data = None
    mailbox_found_path = None
    for mailbox_path in paths_to_check:
        if mailbox_path.exists():
            try:
                with open(mailbox_path) as f:
                    mailbox_data = json.load(f)
                mailbox_found_path = str(mailbox_path)
                break
            except Exception as e:
                continue

    if mailbox_data is None:
        return {'pass': False, 'score': 0.0, 'feedback': 'mailbox.json not found or unreadable in workspace or external services paths.'}

    emails = mailbox_data.get('emails', [])
    if not emails:
        return {'pass': False, 'score': 0.0, 'feedback': f'No emails found in mailbox.json at {mailbox_found_path}.'}

    # Look through all emails for the best match (not just IDs 3 or 4)
    best_score = -1
    best_result = None

    for email in emails:
        eid = str(email.get('email_id', ''))
        
        # Get body_text or similar fields
        body_text = email.get('body_text', '') or ''
        body = email.get('body', '') or ''
        content = email.get('content', '') or ''
        
        # Combine all potential body fields
        all_text = body_text + ' ' + body + ' ' + content
        
        # Normalize for comparison: collapse whitespace variations
        def normalize(s):
            # Replace \n with newline if it's a literal string
            s = s.strip()
            return s
        
        norm_body = normalize(body_text)
        norm_expected = normalize(expected_body)
        
        # Check for exact match first
        exact_match = False
        if norm_body == norm_expected:
            exact_match = True
        
        # Also try a looser comparison: strip all whitespace and compare
        def strip_all_ws(s):
            return re.sub(r'\s+', '', s)
        
        loose_match = strip_all_ws(norm_body) == strip_all_ws(norm_expected)
        
        if exact_match:
            return {
                'pass': True,
                'score': 1.0,
                'feedback': f'Exact body_text match found in email ID {eid} in mailbox.json. All required content (9 claims, variance totals, template placeholders, signature) verified.'
            }
        
        if loose_match:
            return {
                'pass': True,
                'score': 1.0,
                'feedback': f'Body_text match found in email ID {eid} (whitespace-normalized) in mailbox.json. All required content verified.'
            }
        
        # Partial credit: check key fragments in all_text
        found = []
        missing = []
        for fragment, label in key_fragments:
            if fragment in all_text:
                found.append(label)
            else:
                missing.append(label)
        
        score = len(found) / len(key_fragments) if key_fragments else 0.0
        
        if score > best_score:
            best_score = score
            if not missing:
                best_result = {
                    'pass': True,
                    'score': 1.0,
                    'feedback': f'All {len(key_fragments)} key content fragments found in email ID {eid} body. Draft email contains all required claims, variance data, and template elements.'
                }
            else:
                best_result = {
                    'pass': False,
                    'score': round(score, 3),
                    'feedback': f'Email ID {eid} partially matches. Found {len(found)}/{len(key_fragments)} key fragments. Missing: {missing}. Found: {found}.'
                }

    if best_result:
        return best_result
    else:
        return {'pass': False, 'score': 0.0, 'feedback': 'No draft email found in mailbox.json matching the required dispute email template.'}
